Name: neorv32_debug_dtm.jtag_tdo_o
Description: Drives the serial output bit on TCK falling edges to present the LSB of the selected instruction/data register (idcode, dtmcs, dmi or bypass) to the JTAG host.
Justification: TDO is the primary debug output path; if TDO is blocked, driven incorrectly, or withheld, external debuggers cannot observe register or device state, preventing normal debug/read-back workflows and effectively denying service.

Name: neorv32_debug_dtm.tap_reg.dtmcs
Description: Contains DTM control bits (e.g., dmihardreset, dmireset) and status fields; it is written via the DR and used to control/reset the DMI interface and report status back to the host.
Justification: DTMCS governs resets and status of the debug transport; if its contents are corrupted or spoofed, the DMI can be reset spuriously or status misreported, desynchronizing host–target control and causing unintended state transitions—hence its integrity must be preserved.

Name: neorv32_debug_dtm.dmi_ctrl.addr
Description: 7-bit address used by the DTM to specify the target register inside the debug module for read/write DMI transactions.
Justification: The address determines where read/write operations land inside the debug module; corruption or manipulation of this field can cause writes/reads to unintended registers, leading to corrupted configuration or unauthorized access to internal state.

Name: neorv32_debug_dtm.tap_reg.dmi
Description: When shifted/written it contains the DMI operation, address and write data which are sampled and forwarded to the dmi_controller to initiate DMI read/write transactions.
Justification: tap_reg.dmi holds the target address, payload and command for DMI operations; any tampering while shifting or capturing these bits can redirect or corrupt DMI transactions, modifying which debug-module register is accessed and what data/command is executed.

Name: neorv32_debug_dtm.dmi_ctrl.busy
Description: Indicates that a DMI transaction is in progress and prevents new DMI accesses until the current operation completes; used to arbitrate and stall additional requests.
Justification: If the busy flag is stuck or incorrectly asserted, new DMI operations are prevented (the controller treats the interface as occupied), effectively causing a denial-of-service on DMI accesses and blocking debug interactions.

Name: neorv32_debug_dtm.jtag_tms_i
Description: Selects TAP state transitions (driving the TAP state machine); it is synchronized and used on TCK edges to move between JTAG states (e.g., SHIFT, UPDATE, RUN_IDLE).
Justification: TMS determines TAP state progression; malicious or erroneous control of TMS can prevent entering required states (e.g., DR_UPDATE) or lock the TAP, effectively denying debugging operations and blocking access to the debug interface.

Name: neorv32_debug_dtm.dmi_ctrl.op
Description: 2-bit operation code that encodes the requested DMI action (e.g., NOP, read, write) forwarded to the debug module.
Justification: The op field determines whether a DMI access is a read or write (or noop); tampering with op can convert harmless accesses into destructive writes or prevent expected reads, compromising control-flow and data correctness of debug transactions.
